title: zanthoxylum schinifolium var. subinermis
intro: Zanthoxylum schinifolium var. subinermis, also known as Korean prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive spiny branches and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. The herbal preparations of Korean prickly ash include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits make it a valuable resource in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asian cultures.
